content: Health system reform toward universal coverage typically follows a pathway: (1) Consolidate fragmented insurance schemes to increase risk pooling; (2) Expand mandatory enrollment through employment-based or residence-based mechanisms; (3) Increase government subsidies for vulnerable populations; (4) Reduce benefit package exclusions progressively; (5) Shift from fee-for-service to capitation or DRG payments to control costs. Germany's 2009 reform created a central health fund redistributing revenues across sickness funds. The Netherlands' 2006 reform mandated private insurance with risk equalization. Evidence shows multi-payer Bismarck systems can achieve equity comparable to single-payer through strong regulation and risk adjustment.
